a_image.alt: Leather & Stitching UI style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Craft

sections.a_description: Rich saddle brown with visible stitching along every edge — the unmistakable texture of a handcrafted leather journal, tooled and worn to perfection. Leather & Stitching UI brings the artisanal quality of a master leatherworker's craft to the screen, with embossed text that looks pressed into the hide and borders that trace the path of a saddler's needle.

This aesthetic appears on luxury leather goods shops, bespoke tailor sites, and craft workshop portfolios. You'll find it on whiskey brand pages, men's grooming sites, heritage boot companies, and any brand that wants their digital presence to feel as hand-crafted and premium as a made-to-order leather briefcase.

a_image_cover.alt: Leather & Stitching UI style use case illustration
Leather & Stitching UI style use case illustration
Leather & Stitching UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: Rich, tooled leather with visible stitching — the premium tactile quality of a handcrafted leather journal or wallet. Background: deep saddle brown (#3e2a1a) with a subtle leather grain texture via pseudo-element. The .inner panel is a lighter leather patch: warm tan (#c8a882) with visible stitching around the border — created via a 2px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.4) border set 8px inward (using outline + outline-offset, or a nested border approach). The leather has a subtle radial gradient for a slight sheen (lighter in the center, darker at edges). Text is embossed — dark brown with subtle lighter text-shadow below for that pressed-into-leather look. Set in Bitter for headings — a slab serif whose sturdy, somewhat industrial character mirrors the tooled, structured nature of leather goods. Body in Inter for readability. Heading in Bitter at weight 700, 28px, line-height 1.2, color #2c1a0e, text-shadow 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.15) for embossed effect. Eyebrow in Inter at 11px, weight 600, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #8b6f47. Body in Inter at 15px, weight 400, line-height 1.6, color #3e2a1a. Buttons: primary — dark brown (#3e2a1a), tan text, 6px radius, inset shadow for pressed look. Secondary: transparent, stitched dashed border. Alternatives: Roboto Slab, Zilla Slab, Arvo.
